what_it_does: Records ledger metadata for an image asset in the design-source channel and labels it as source design ground-truth.
observations: YAML frontmatter holds image metadata: id, address, channel, path, blob URI, mime type, name, width, height, source, author_session, created timestamp, and empty links.
Width and height are both 0 even though mime is image/jpeg.
The body contains only the line 'SOURCE design ground-truth — recent-pitches/p-32.jpg'.
standouts: Width and height are reported as 0 for a JPEG image.
The file explicitly marks the asset as 'SOURCE design ground-truth'.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a stable id/address/channel/path/blob/mime schema.
Uses a markdown body with a terse SOURCE label.
concerns: Zero width and height may indicate missing or unextracted image dimensions.
No links or references are provided, so the asset stands isolated in this file.
notes: This is a minimal, addressable ledger card for a design-source image asset that also asserts it is ground-truth material.
questions: What visual content does recent-pitches/p-32.jpg contain?
What downstream artifact or pitch is this image intended to ground?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a metadata record for a design-source image asset, and the body reinforces the source-ground-truth role.
intention_signals: Catalog a specific design image as an authoritative source asset and make it addressable within the design-source channel.
contribution: Provides a stable, addressable metadata record and blob pointer for an image asset, plus an explicit source-ground-truth label that downstream fusion can treat as a provenance anchor.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger metadata file for the design-source image asset recent-pitches/p-32.jpg, including blob reference, mime type, zero dimensions, and a source-ground-truth label.
intention_for_embedding: To register and mark a design image as authoritative ground-truth material within the design-source channel.
